refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional objective: supporting achievement of a cycle time target for a specific operational cycle. It does not include multiple obligations or implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load mechanism speed and efficiency
Optimize travel mechanism speed and path
Optimize dump mechanism speed and efficiency
Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time
Implement cycle time monitoring and adaptive control to meet target
selected_dp.selected_dp: Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time

potential_sources: Interdependence of load, travel, and dump operations timing
coupling_check.notes: Selecting coordination as the DP explicitly addresses the integration of sub-operations to meet the overall cycle time target, reducing coupling by managing dependencies at the operational level.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that load, travel, and dump mechanisms can be controlled and coordinated effectively
Risk that external factors (e.g., site conditions, operator behavior) may impact cycle time despite coordination
Assumes accurate measurement and feedback of cycle time for adaptive control
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for cycle time monitoring and adaptive control to handle variability and ensure meeting the p95 cycle time target under varying conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system coordination logic, including scheduling and sequencing of load, travel, and dump operations to optimize efficiency and minimize idle time.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives status updates from Vehicle and sends operational commands.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-level task assignments and operational goals.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety alerts from Safety Services to adjust operations accordingly.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with Teleoperation and other external systems for coordination and control.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as coordination logic naturally belongs here; minimal diffusion since it centralizes scheduling.
Vehicle module handles physical operations but coordination across multiple vehicles would diffuse logic outside.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not overall coordination; would cause high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on safety monitoring, not operational coordination; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP as it centralizes coordination logic, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth in NGHE Solution if coordination logic becomes too large.
Dependency on timely and reliable interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) for effective coordination.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying coordination of load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time, directly supporting achievement of the customer-defined cycle time target.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on multiple external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) introduces some risk if coordination logic is spread across modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and centralizes the coordination logic, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment of the DP. Other modules show poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting this allocation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and ensuring operational policies and safety plans are followed.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The NGHE Solution module clearly owns the coordination logic implementing the DP, with well-defined interfaces to other modules for status, commands, and safety alerts.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth in the NGHE Solution module to avoid maintainability issues as coordination logic expands.
Ensure robust and timely communication over external interfaces to prevent coordination delays impacting cycle time.
Consider modularizing coordination sub-functions within NGHE Solution if complexity becomes high to maintain clarity and manageability.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical support, good containment, and feasible verification. The coordination logic is centralized in the NGHE Solution module, minimizing diffusion risks. Although there are recommendations to monitor complexity and communication robustness, these are operational considerations rather than indications to further decompose the FR. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and manageability is important, but current decomposition is sufficient to proceed without further sub-FRs.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will maintain high cohesion and manage complexity effectively.
External interfaces will remain robust and timely to support coordination logic.
open_risks: Dependency on multiple external interfaces may impact coordination if communication delays occur.
Potential complexity growth in the NGHE Solution module could affect maintainability.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data on cycle time p95 under operational conditions.
Metrics on module complexity and communication latency over time.
